name: LibCST (Concrete Syntax Tree for Python)

performance.notes: Pure Python with Rust optional acceleration; parses Python source to CST

agent_readiness.notes: Concrete Syntax Tree for Python — preserves all whitespace, comments, formatting. import libcst as cst. tree = cst.parse_module('def foo(): pass'). tree.code → original source. Visitor: class MyVisitor(cst.CSTVisitor): def visit_FunctionDef(self, node): .... Transformer: class MyTransformer(cst.CSTTransformer): def leave_FunctionDef(self, original, updated): return updated.with_changes(name=cst.Name('new_name')). Wrapper: from libcst.metadata import MetadataWrapper, QualifiedNameProvider. wrapper = MetadataWrapper(tree). Codemods: libcst.codemod for large-scale refactoring. Used by Instagram for Python codemod at scale. codemod run -x MyTransform src/.

gotchas: Python-only — no multi-language support (use tree-sitter for polyglot)
CST is more verbose than ast module — every whitespace is a node
Metadata providers require MetadataWrapper — adds complexity
Lossless round-trip is the key feature but adds API complexity
best_when: Automated Python code refactoring that preserves formatting and comments
When ast module loses comments/formatting and you need lossless transforms
Type annotation migrations, import reorganization, API deprecation tools
avoid_when: Simple AST analysis where formatting doesn't matter (use ast module)
Reading code structure without transforming it
summary: LibCST provides a lossless Concrete Syntax Tree for Python — preserves whitespace, comments, formatting. cst.parse_module(code). CSTVisitor for reading, CSTTransformer for modifying. MetadataWrapper for type/scope resolution. Codemods for large-scale refactoring. MIT by Instagram/Meta. Best for automated Python refactoring that preserves formatting. More verbose than ast module. Python-only. Used for import migrations, API deprecation, type annotation additions.
